response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and systems engineer whose career has been built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month role at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the 5–10 years of Windows-platform C++ required. He has no demonstrable fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge; his one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) used TypeScript/GraphQL. SQL experience is listed generically with no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, and desirable skills such as Summit, STL, Python, Kafka/MQ are absent entirely.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated low-latency and high-throughput systems work (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic).
Strong CI/CD, DevOps, and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, pairing) evidenced across multiple roles.
Active public presence — open-source projects, conference talks, and workshops — indicating solid communication and knowledge-sharing ability.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible relative to the 5–10 year mandatory requirement; primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang.
Zero fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge — the single finance role used an entirely different technology stack.
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; database experience skews toward MongoDB and generic SQL.
